name: Plumber Bros.
description: Side-scrolling plumber adventure for the TurboNova. Every kid had a copy. Loose cart, no box.

name: Duck Blast
description: Point-and-shoot duck hunting game. Requires the Light Blaster accessory. Dirt cheap and everywhere.

name: Mega Dude
description: Robot hero blasts through eight themed stages. Tough as nails. Loose TurboNova cart.

name: Legend of Eldra
description: Top-down adventure with dungeons, puzzles, and a golden triangle. A TurboNova essential. Loose cart.

name: Castlefang
description: Whip-wielding vampire hunter storms a gothic castle. Low print run makes this a collector favorite. Loose cart.

name: Velocity Hedgehog
description: Blue critter blazes through loop-de-loops at breakneck speed. The MegaDrive SX's mascot game. Loose cart.

name: Super Plumber Land
description: The plumber's 16-bit debut. Vibrant worlds, secret exits, rideable dinosaur. Loose MegaDrive SX cart.

name: Street Brawler II Turbo
description: The definitive 16-bit fighting game. Quarter-circle motions burned into muscle memory. Loose cart.

name: Final Odyssey III
description: Epic 40-hour RPG with a villain everyone loves to hate. Battery save still holds if you're lucky. Loose cart.

name: Chrono Fable
description: Time-travel RPG with multiple endings. Late-era MegaDrive SX gem. Limited production run keeps prices high. Loose cart.

name: Starwing Assault
description: On-rails space shooter with a custom graphics chip in the cart. Low distribution outside Japan. A true grail piece. Loose cart.

name: Plumber's Quest 3D
description: The plumber leaps into 3D. Revolutionary analog stick controls. Everyone's first Prism 64 game. Loose cart.

name: Kart Championship
description: Four-player split-screen kart racing. Friendships tested, controllers thrown. Loose Prism 64 cart.

name: Eldra: Realm of Time
description: 3D action-adventure with a time-travel mechanic and a legendary soundtrack. The Eldra series at its peak. Loose cart.

name: Agent Zero: Golden Scope
description: Four-player split-screen spy shooter. No screen-peeking allowed. A Prism 64 multiplayer legend. Loose cart.

name: Velocity Hedgehog (CIB)
description: Cart, box, and manual. Box corners show some wear but the insert art is intact. Complete MegaDrive SX package.

name: Legend of Eldra (CIB)
description: TurboNova cart with original box, manual, and fold-out map. The map alone is worth $5 to the right buyer.

name: Chrono Fable (CIB)
description: Complete with cart, box, manual, and registration card. Already rare loose — CIB copies are scarce. A centerpiece item.

name: Castlefang (NIB)
description: Factory sealed TurboNova Castlefang. Original shrink wrap intact. How did this survive 30+ years? Glass case material.

name: MegaDrive SX Console (For Parts)
description: Powers on but no video output. Cosmetic damage on the shell. Good candidate for a refurbishment project.

name: TurboNova Console (For Parts)
description: No power LED. Cartridge slot is bent. Someone tried to fix it with tape. Worth saving if you know what you're doing.

name: Prism 64 Console (For Parts)
description: Expansion port is corroded and the controller ports are loose. Internals might still be good. A project for the patient.

name: MegaDrive SX Controller (For Parts)
description: D-pad is mushy and the B button sticks. Cable has a kink near the plug. Parts donor or restoration project.

name: TurboNova Console (Working)
description: Tested and working TurboNova with power cable and one controller. Some yellowing on the shell but runs perfectly.

name: Prism 64 Console (NIB)
description: Factory sealed Prism 64 in the original Midnight Black colorway. Seal is intact, box has minor shelf wear. A collector's dream.

name: TurboNova Controller
description: Standard rectangular TurboNova controller. D-pad and two buttons. Feels like holding a brick, but that's the charm.

name: Prism 64 Memory Unit
description: Plugs into the controller for game saves. 256Kb capacity. Someone else's RPG saves are probably still on it.

name: MegaDrive SX A/V Cable
description: Composite A/V cable for the MegaDrive SX. Red, white, and yellow — the holy trinity of retro video. Low margin, always needed.

name: TurboNova Light Blaster
description: Orange plastic light gun for the TurboNova. Required for Duck Blast. Only works on CRT displays — a fun talking point.

name: Official Eldra Strategy Guide
description: Full-color walkthrough with maps for every dungeon. Spine is cracked from use, but all pages present. A nostalgia trip.

name: Power Surge Magazine Issue #47
description: Launch issue covering the Prism 64 debut. Includes a pull-out poster and first-look reviews of Plumber's Quest 3D. Collector item.
